   "source": [
    "# Noise Generation: Basics\n",
    "\n",
    "**Goal**: Learn how to generate, manipulate, and model noise in gravitational-wave data using **GWexpy**.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Prerequisites**:\n",
    "- `pygwinc` (optional, for detector models)\n",
    "- `obspy` (optional, for seismic models)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "5a880e4a",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.013535,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:30.184323+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:30.170788+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 1. Generating ASD (Amplitude Spectral Density)\n",
    "\n",
    "GWexpy provides several ways to generate ASDs, from theoretical power-laws to detector-specific models."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "15287765",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-25T13:24:30.249254Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-25T13:24:30.248966Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-25T13:24:42.009237Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-25T13:24:42.007950Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 11.777813,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:42.010017+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:30.232204+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import warnings\n",
    "\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=UserWarning)\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\", category=DeprecationWarning)\n",
    "\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy.noise import asd\n",
    "\n",
    "freqs = np.logspace(0, 3, 1000)\n",
    "\n",
    "# 1. Power-law ASDs\n",
    "white = asd.white_noise(amplitude=1e-23, frequencies=freqs)\n",
    "pink = asd.pink_noise(amplitude=1e-22, f_ref=10, frequencies=freqs)\n",
    "red = asd.red_noise(amplitude=1e-21, f_ref=10, frequencies=freqs)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.loglog(white.xindex, white, label=\"White (f^0)\")\n",
    "plt.loglog(pink.xindex, pink, label=\"Pink (f^-0.5)\")\n",
    "plt.loglog(red.xindex, red, label=\"Red (f^-1)\")\n",
    "plt.ylabel(\"ASD [strain/rtHz]\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b8ecc42b",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.023804,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:42.044415+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:42.020611+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 2. Generating Time-domain Noise\n",
    "\n",
    "You can generate colored noise directly in the time domain using `gwexpy.noise.wave`."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "2f2dbe6d",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-25T13:24:42.124855Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-25T13:24:42.123968Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-25T13:24:58.829237Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-25T13:24:58.828334Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 16.735634,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:58.831828+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:42.096194+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 1200x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "from gwexpy.noise import wave\n",
    "\n",
    "duration = 4.0\n",
    "fs = 2048\n",
    "\n",
    "# Generate pink noise time series\n",
    "noise_ts = wave.pink_noise(duration=duration, sample_rate=fs, amplitude=1e-21)\n",
    "\n",
    "noise_ts.plot()\n",
    "plt.title(\"Colored Noise Time Series\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "bd720dfc",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.124195,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:58.977759+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:58.853564+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 3. Spectral Lines and Masking\n",
    "\n",
    "Real noise contains narrow resonance peaks (lines). You can add them using Lorentzian or Gaussian shapes."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "1320765e",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-25T13:24:59.060149Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-25T13:24:59.057694Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-25T13:25:00.579860Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-25T13:25:00.578521Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 1.549453,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:00.582006+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:24:59.032553+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "from gwexpy.noise.asd import lorentzian_line\n",
    "\n",
    "# lorentzian_line requires Q or gamma (HWHM)\n",
    "line = lorentzian_line(f0=60, gamma=1.0, amplitude=1e-21, frequencies=freqs)\n",
    "total_asd = white + line\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.loglog(freqs, white.value, \"--\", label=\"Background\")\n",
    "plt.loglog(freqs, total_asd.value, label=\"With 60Hz Line\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "3bc375d0",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.011633,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:00.616832+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:00.605199+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 4. Non-Gaussian Noise (Glitches)\n",
    "\n",
    "`gwexpy.noise.non_gaussian` provides models for non-stationary noise like scattered light or transient bursts."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "4a42f263",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-25T13:25:00.714584Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-25T13:25:00.714035Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-25T13:25:02.104094Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-25T13:25:02.098102Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 1.412497,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:02.106528+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:00.694031+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x800 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "from gwexpy.noise.non_gaussian import scatter_light_noise, transient_gaussian_noise\n",
    "\n",
    "# 1. Transient burst (Model I)\n",
    "burst = transient_gaussian_noise(duration=1.0, sample_rate=fs, A1=10.0)\n",
    "\n",
    "# 2. Scattered light (Model II)\n",
    "scatter = scatter_light_noise(duration=10.0, sample_rate=fs, A2=1e-6, G=1e-21)\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(10, 8))\n",
    "ax[0].plot(burst.times, burst); ax[0].set_title(\"Transient Gaussian Burst\")\n",
    "ax[1].plot(scatter.times, scatter); ax[1].set_title(\"Scattered Light Noise\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "ce6c178b",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.025011,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:02.157015+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:02.132004+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## 5. Case Study: Simple Noise Budget\n",
    "\n",
    "Combine multiple noise sources (seismic, thermal, shot noise) to create a total noise budget."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "a534a444",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-25T13:25:02.252127Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-25T13:25:02.251855Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-25T13:25:03.541700Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-25T13:25:03.540671Z"
    },
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 1.317606,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:03.546868+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:02.229262+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": []
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 640x480 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# seismic ~ f^-2\n",
    "seismic = asd.power_law(exponent=2, amplitude=1e-20, f_ref=10, frequencies=freqs)\n",
    "# shot ~ f^0\n",
    "shot = asd.white_noise(amplitude=1e-23, frequencies=freqs)\n",
    "\n",
    "total = np.sqrt(seismic.value**2 + shot.value**2)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.loglog(freqs, seismic.value, label=\"Seismic\")\n",
    "plt.loglog(freqs, shot.value, label=\"Shot\")\n",
    "plt.loglog(freqs, total, \"k\", lw=2, label=\"Total\")\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "4af7d682",
   "metadata": {
    "papermill": {
     "duration": 0.026271,
     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:03.595715+00:00",
     "exception": false,
     "start_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:03.569444+00:00",
     "status": "completed"
    },
    "tags": [
     "lang-en"
    ]
   },
   "source": [
    "## Common Mistakes and Failure Modes\n",
    "\n",
    "- **Assuming the ASD slope alone validates the time-domain realization**: a generated noise series can match the target trend on average while still differing in finite-length variance, line structure, or transient content.\n",
    "- **Reading synthetic components as independent just because they were generated separately**: when you combine seismic, thermal, and shot-noise-like terms, the total curve is a modeling construction, not evidence that real detector subsystems are separable that way.\n",
    "- **Confusing line amplitude with line width or quality factor**: for narrow spectral features, changing `Q` or `gamma` affects interpretation differently from changing overall amplitude.\n",
    "- **Over-interpreting glitches as realistic populations**: the non-Gaussian examples illustrate shapes and workflow behavior, not a calibrated model of real glitch rates or detector environments.\n",
    "- **Comparing curves without checking units and reference frequencies**: a visually reasonable power law can still be mis-parameterized if `amplitude`, `exponent`, or `f_ref` are interpreted inconsistently.\n",
    "- **Treating a simple noise budget as a validated instrument budget**: the case study shows how to combine terms numerically, but it is not a substitute for validated coupling measurements or full uncertainty accounting.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "de9db83d",
   "metadata": {

     "end_time": "2026-04-25T13:25:03.683468+00:00",

   },
   "source": [
    "## 6. Exercises\n",
    "\n",
    "1. **Spectrum-to-Time**: Use `wave.colored` to generate a 10-second signal with an exponent of 1.5, then plot its ASD to verify the slope.\n",
    "2. **Line Removal**: (Challenge) Remove the 60Hz line generated in Section 3."
   ]
  },
